name: SAL.js
description: Lightweight scroll animation library — pure IntersectionObserver, zero dependencies, 2.5kb. Data-attribute driven like AOS but 3x smaller with no scroll event polling.
use_cases: alternative to AOS on performance-critical pages
head:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/sal.js@0.9.0/dist/sal.css">
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/sal.js@0.9.0/dist/sal.js"></script>
body:
<script>
// SAL.js usage — add data attributes to HTML elements:
// data-sal="fade"           — fade in
// data-sal="slide-up"       — slide up
// data-sal="slide-down"     — slide down
// data-sal="slide-left"     — slide from left
// data-sal="slide-right"    — slide from right
// data-sal="zoom-in"        — scale up
// data-sal-duration="700"   — animation duration ms
// data-sal-delay="200"      — delay ms
// data-sal-easing="ease-out-cubic"

(function() {
  if (typeof sal === 'undefined') return;
  sal({
    threshold: 0.15,
    once: true,
    disable: false
  });
})();
</script>
